# Thronglets

> AI-first decision substrate for coding agents

Thronglets helps an agent leave and reuse sparse execution signals.

It is optimized for:
- low-latency prehook guidance
- silence by default
- machine-facing bootstrap and health checks
- local-first learning with optional collective corroboration

## Install

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Shangri-la-0428/Thronglets/main/scripts/install.sh | sh
npm install -g thronglets
thronglets start

## Known Local Adapters

- Claude Code
- Codex
- OpenClaw

`thronglets start` configures known adapters, runs a bootstrap health pass, and reports one next step.

For a second device:

thronglets join --file ./thronglets.connection.json

## Machine Bootstrap

Agents should not parse long prose to configure Thronglets. Use the contract:

thronglets detect --json
thronglets install-plan --agent codex --json
thronglets apply-plan --agent codex --json
thronglets doctor --agent codex --json

Or use the single-step path:

thronglets bootstrap --agent codex --json

The machine schema is:

thronglets.bootstrap.v2

All bootstrap commands return:
- data.summary first
- details second

## Universal Hook Contract

Unknown runtimes should use:

thronglets install-plan --agent generic --json

That returns canonical examples for:
- prehook stdin
- hook stdin

## Runtime Behavior

The live prehook emits at most a few sparse signals:
- avoid
- do next
- maybe also
- context

The post-tool hook records traces and local workspace learning.

## Operator Commands

thronglets profile-summary
thronglets profile-check
thronglets eval-signals --json
thronglets release-check --eval-scope both --json

## Network

thronglets run --bootstrap /ip4/47.93.32.88/tcp/4001

## Seed Node

/ip4/47.93.32.88/tcp/4001
